rulings.Case Title: MARIANNE CUNNINGHAM et al VS. GOLDEN GATE HEALTH CENTER et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn For Terminating Sanctions/Alt To Compel Independent Medical Examination And For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, December 6, 2012, Line 2, DEFENDANT KINDRED NURSING CENTER WEST LLC DBA GOLDEN Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn For Terminating Sanctions/Alt To Compel Independent Medical Examination And For Sanctions Pro Tem Judge Nils Rosenquest, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Hearing required. Parties are to meet and confer on date, time, and place for IME. Suggested parameters for IME are (a) no later than December 21, 2012 and (b) within five (5) miles of Plaintiff's residence in the West Bay/Peninsula area. =302/jpt

rulings.Case Title: DAVID GIZZARELLI VS. SAN FRANCISCO CITY & COUNTY
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petitioner'S Application For Writ Of Mandamus
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, December 6, 2012, Line 21, Petitioner'S Application For Writ Of Mandamus. The petition for writ of mandate is denied. Even if respondent failed to provide petitioner with written notice of the hearing (the evidence is conflicting, see AR 44 and 50; Petitioner's reply declaration), petitioner waived the defect by appearing at the hearing and arguing the merits. Substantial evidence supported respondent's decision. Petitioner's argument that the decision was based on triple hearsay is incorrect. Officer Evans witnessed the attack and testified at the hearing. The hearing complied with the SF Health Code and did not violate Cal. Food & Agricultural Code § 31621. The standard of proof at the hearing under the SF Health Code and Cal. Food & Agricultural Code § 31621 is preponderance of the evidence. The standard of review before this Court is substantial evidence. See Zuniga v. San Mateo Dept. of Health Services (1990) 218 Cal.App.3d 1521, 1530-1531. While the Court finds Officer Denny's multi-faceted role in this case as witness, interviewer, and hearing officer troubling, Petitioner did not timely raise that objection at the hearing or demonstrate bias. Petitioner fails to demonstrate an abuse of discretion. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. = (302/AJR)

rulings.Case Title: JANE GELFAND VS. POSITIVE RESOURCE CENTER, A CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Compel Production Of Documents
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, December 6, 2012, Line 6, Notice Of Motion To Compel Production Of Documents Pro Tem Judge Nils Rosenquest, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Hearing required. At the hearing it is requested that parties address (1) whether or not a pre-production protective order or stipulation was sought concerning the form of production, (2) why documents numbered PRC000624-916 were not watermarked, (3) why the alphanumeric bates stamp does not furnish sufficient identification as to the source of the document, and (4) the form in which the documents are kept in the ordinary course of business (the Court understands that the watermark was added pursuant to production and not inserted electronically in the ordinary course of business). =302/jpt

rulings.Case Title: CHARLES LEE VS. MOROSO CONSTRUCTION INC., A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Mtn For Leave To File First Amended Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, December 6, 2012, Line 17, PLAINTIFFS CHARLES LEE and ALEXANDRA BOYLE'S Motion For Leave To File First Amended Complaint. Granted as to the prayer for relief for punitive damages and denied for a $ 500 penalty for each and every violation of Business & Professions Code section 7160. For punitive damages, prejudice will not result from amendment. Plaintiff originally asserted a fraud claim upon which punitive damages may be sought. For Business & Professions Code section § 7160, the plain language of § 7160 refers to a single penalty of $ 500 for multiple misrepresentations and statements made to induce a party to contract for a work of improvement. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests.
